content:
- read all .ralph task files and evidence logs, then manually reviewed every source/test file under src/ and tests/ (no subagent capability available)
- ran fresh verification gates with CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1: make check, make test, make test-bdd, make lint, make test-real; all passed; confirmed no unwrap/expect/panic and no #[ignore] tests
- emailed a full skeptical report to the PO, noting real-binary tests are conditional unless test-real is enforced and e2e test uses explicit DCS writes to drive scenarios
- should do next, after quitting immediately due to context limit: wait for PO response or follow-up requests

- Read src and tests across config, state, pginfo, dcs, process, ha, api, debug_api, and test_harness to build a full understanding of system behavior.
- Sent multiple status emails to PO and delivered a comprehensive narrative report with headings and paragraphs, based strictly on code evidence, without running builds/tests or binaries.
- Next time: wait for further instructions; if asked for follow‑ups, revisit any specific module or tests and expand the report as needed.

- Read all test files in tests/ and src/ to inventory every #[test]/#[tokio::test] case without running tests.
- Extracted and summarized each test scenario, plus identified real-binary skip behavior tied to PGTUSKMASTER_REQUIRE_REAL_BINARIES and missing .tools binaries.
- Emailed PO multiple progress updates and delivered a full narrative report with headings and exhaustive scenario coverage plus gating notes.
- Next: none pending unless PO requests follow-up or clarifications.

- used add-task-as-agent skill and created 9 new not_started story tasks (22-30) under .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness covering: full HA admin API, API CLI, 3-node+3-etcd e2e topology, API-only/no-direct-DCS e2e policy guard, unassisted failover SQL consistency e2e, HA stress workload e2e, network partition chaos e2e, TLS adversarial tests, and black-box API/CLI orchestration migration
- verified the task files exist and aligned each to the requested skeptical requirements and acceptance-gate template
- next after quitting due context: continue Ralph loop was requested, so run continue-ralph sequence (systemctl check/stop if active, git add/clean cached secrets/logs, commit, task switch, restart via timeout --signal=QUIT)

- used add-task-as-agent skill to create 3 extensive documentation tasks in .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness: 31 framework+install+gitignore hygiene, 32 architecture docs authoring with diagrams/no code-level narration, 33 deep skeptical factual+editorial validation
- encoded explicit hard requirements from user into acceptance criteria, especially artifact hygiene (observe generated outputs first, ignore correctly, verify via git add checks, clean staged/tracked artifacts and branch history if accidentally committed)
- chained tasks with blocked_by links (32 -> 31, 33 -> 32) to enforce correct sequence from platform setup to writing to truth/quality validation
- should do next: execute task 31 with real framework comparison and install/build proof, then produce architecture docs and final skeptical verification report

- refined task 31 wording per user feedback: now explicitly sets VitePress as preferred baseline due to aesthetics and forbids choosing an uglier framework
- added hard gate: any non-VitePress selection must provide side-by-side evidence proving equal-or-better visual quality/UX before acceptance

- Read prior progress log (no prior output visible this run).
- Parsed `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and applied selection rules in order.
- Verified all bug tasks are already passing (`<passes>true</passes>` / `<passing>true</passing>`), so bug-first condition did not force a bug pick.
- Found multiple non-bug tasks with `<passes>false</passes>`; these outrank the recurring `<passes>meta-task</passes>` per instructions.
- Evaluated unblock graph among remaining false-pass tasks: unblocked roots are 22 (HA admin API), 24 (3-node/3-etcd harness), and 31 (docs framework).
- Picked `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/22-task-ha-admin-api-read-write-surface.md` as most logical next task because it is earliest in the active chain and unlocks several downstream tasks (23, 25, 29, 30 and their dependents).
- Wrote selected path to `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stopped.

- loaded Ralph memory, read .ralph/current_task.txt, and inspected task 22 state (no lifecycle marker present) so stayed strictly in planning phase
- ran broad parallel exploration across 12+ tracks covering api controller/worker/mod, config schema/defaults/parser, bdd api tests, dcs typed writer helpers, and e2e direct-dcs usage to anchor a concrete implementation strategy
- wrote a full detailed implementation plan into .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/22-task-ha-admin-api-read-write-surface.md, including endpoint contract shape, auth/config wiring, test matrix, skeptical verification instructions, and ended with TO BE VERIFIED as required
- should do next after immediate quit: when this task is picked again in TO BE VERIFIED phase, run deep skeptical verification with 16+ parallel tracks, alter at least one plan item with rationale, replace marker with NOW EXECUTE, then execute without further exploration
